findings.evidence: `chafaiDensity` is `(-1)^n/(n-1)! * t^(n-1) * iteratedDerivWithin n f` at lines 73-76, `chafaiRescaled` is only `Measure.map (chafaiRescaling n) (chafaiMeasure f n)` at line 325, and the mass bound at lines 656-660 uses `C = f 0 - L`, excluding the required atom of mass `L` at zero.
findings.fix: Either define the full approximating/limit measure as the derivative-density part plus the limit atom at `0`, or explicitly rename/scope these measures as approximating only `f - L` and provide the separate `L • δ₀` reconstruction before exposing them as Bernstein representation infrastructure.
findings.issue: `chafaiRescaled` only pushes forward derivative-density measures, which miss the `f(∞)·δ₀` part of the Bernstein representing measure; for a constant completely monotone function `f(t)=c>0`, lines 73-76 make every positive-order density zero and line 325 therefore gives the zero rescaled measures, while the representing measure must be `c δ₀`.

summary: The Chafaï measure infrastructure omits the atom at 0 coming from the limit of a completely monotone function at infinity, so it cannot faithfully serve as Bernstein representing-measure infrastructure as stated.
